title: mespilus cotoneaster subsp. rubra
intro: Mespilus cotoneaster subsp. rubra, also known as the red medlar or red medlars, is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its dark green leaves and red or purple berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Mespilus cotoneaster subsp. rubra include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often used to treat colds, coughs, and respiratory problems.
